title: BossFeature Object
description: Object that represents an existing boss feature in a design. For history free model this interface has limited functionality.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createForAssemblyContext
methods.description: Creates or returns a proxy for the native object - i.e. a new object that represents this object but adds the assembly context defined by the input occurrence.

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates object with inputs this feature represents. To use this property, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True).

methods.name: deleteMe
methods.description: Deletes the feature. This works for both parametric and non-parametric features.

methods.name: dissolve
methods.description: Dissolves the feature so that the feature information is lost and only the B-Rep geometry defined by the feature remains. This is only valid for non-parametric features.

methods.name: update
methods.description: Changes the boss feature (or boss connection) to the input provided. To use this property, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True).

properties.name: alignmentDepth
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling the step depth used for alignment of its counterparts.

properties.name: alignmentDiameter
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling the step diameter used for alignment of its counterparts.

properties.name: alignmentDraftAngle
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling the step draft angle.

properties.name: alignmentRootRadius
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling blend radius of the boss alignment root.

properties.name: alignmentTipRadius
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling blend radius of the boss alignment top face.

properties.name: alignmentType
properties.description: Returns the current boss alignment shape this feature represents.

properties.name: assemblyContext
properties.description: Returns the assembly occurrence (i.e. the occurrence) of this object in an assembly. This is only valid in the case where this is acting as a proxy in an assembly. Returns null in the case where the object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

properties.name: attributes
properties.description: Returns the collection of attributes associated with this face.

properties.name: baseFeature
properties.description: If this feature is associated with a base feature, this property will return that base feature. If it's not associated with a base feature, this property will return null.

properties.name: bodies
properties.description: Returns the bodies that were modified or created by this feature. This property works for both parametric and non-parametric features.   For a BaseFeature, this property has two different behaviors depending on if the BaseFeature is active for edit or not. When the base feature is active, this property returns the bodies owned by the base feature or the source bodies. When the base feature is not active, this property returns the result bodies.   When a body is added to a base feature, that body is owned by the base feature and is only seen in the UI when the base feature is active. This body is referred to as a "source body". Fusion creates a parametric copy of the body when you exit the base feature. This copy is referred to as the "result body," and it is used for subsequent modeling operations.   You can map between the source and result bodies by using their position within the bodies returned. To get a valid list of result bodies, you should roll the timeline to immediately after the base feature node in the timeline. Otherwise, subsequent operations could have done something to cause one or more bodies to no longer be available.

properties.name: diameter
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling the shank diameter.

properties.name: direction
properties.description: Returns the direction of the boss feature with respect to selected position point. For selected sketch point this direction represents a Z-axis of the sketch.

properties.name: draftAngle
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling the shank draft angle.

properties.name: entityToken
properties.description: Returns a token for the Feature object. This can be saved and used at a later time with the Design.findEntityByToken method to get back the same feature.   When using entity tokens it's important to understand that the token string returned for a specific entity can be different over time. However, even if you have two different token strings that were obtained from the same entity, when you use findEntityByToken they will both return the same entity. Because of that you should never compare entity tokens as way to determine what the token represents. Instead, you need to use the findEntityByToken method to get the two entities identified by the tokens and then compare them.

properties.name: errorOrWarningMessage
properties.description: Returns the error or warning message in the case where the healthState property returns either WarningFeatureHealthState or ErrorFeatureHealthState. Otherwise this property returns an empty string.

properties.name: faces
properties.description: Returns the faces that were created by this feature. This works for both parametric and non-parametric features.   Depending on the modifications made to the body after the feature was created, this may not return all of the faces. For example, when a component is created from a body, the original body and its faces no longer exist, so this won't return any faces. However, you can roll the timeline to immediately after the feature, and all the original faces will be available.

properties.name: healthState
properties.description: Returns the current health state of the feature.

properties.name: holeCountersinkAngle
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling countersink angle for countersink hole. If hole type is not set to countersink hole or boss shape is to BossBlank this parameter is unused.

properties.name: holeDepth
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling the hole depth with respect to hole extent type. If hole extent type is set to BossHoleThrough parameter not used. If hole extent type is BossBlindFull the parameter is a distance from farthest face. If hole extent type is set to BossBlindDepth the parameter is a distance from start face of the hole.

properties.name: holeDiameter
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling the hole diameter.

properties.name: holeDraftAngle
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling hole draft angle.

properties.name: holeEndRadius
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling blend radius of the hole end.

properties.name: holeExtentType
properties.description: Returns the current type of hole extent this feature represents.

properties.name: holeMajorDepth
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling major hole depth for counterbore and countersink hole. If hole type is set to simple hole or boss shape is to BossBlank this parameter is unused.

properties.name: holeMajorDiameter
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling major hole diameter for counterbore and countersink hole. If hole type is set to simple hole or boss shape is to BossBlank this parameter is unused.

properties.name: holeMajorDraftAngle
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling major hole draft angle for counterbore and countersink hole. If hole type is set to simple hole or boss shape is to BossBlank this parameter is unused.

properties.name: holeMajorRootRadius
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling blend radius of major hole counterbore root.

properties.name: holeMajorTipRadius
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling blend radius of major hole counterbore.

properties.name: holeStartRadius
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling blend radius of the hole start.

properties.name: holeType
properties.description: Returns the current type of hole this feature represents.

properties.name: innerRadius
properties.description: Returns the model parameter for inner radius - reference for parametric expressions.

properties.name: isDirectionFlipped
properties.description: Gets and sets if the direction of the boss (or boss connection) is flipped.

properties.name: isGeometryOpposite
properties.description: Gets if this boss feature instance represents a bottom side where screw thread engages with the part. If this feature instance represents a geometry where screw head engages it returns false.

properties.name: isParametric
properties.description: Indicates if this feature is parametric or not.

properties.name: isSuppressed
properties.description: Gets and sets if this feature is suppressed. This is only valid for parametric features.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: linkedFeatures
properties.description: Returns the set of features that are linked to this feature. The set of linked features are all of the features that were created in various components as the result of a single feature being created in the user interface.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Returns the name of the feature as seen in the browser (non-parametric) or in the timeline (parametric).

properties.name: nativeObject
properties.description: The NativeObject is the object outside the context of an assembly and in the context of its parent component. Returns null in the case where this object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: offset
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling the offset from the selected parting plane.

properties.name: offsetClearance
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling the offset clearance from the selected parting plane and offset.

properties.name: parentComponent
properties.description: Returns the parent component that owns this feature.

properties.name: positionDefinition
properties.description: Returns a BossPositionDefinition object that provides access to the information used to define the position of the boss feature.

properties.name: ribBlendRadius
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling size of rib root blend radius.

properties.name: ribChamferAngle
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling size of rib chamfer angle.

properties.name: ribCount
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling number of ribs.

properties.name: ribCutSize
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling size of rib chamfer or fillet.

properties.name: ribDraftAngle
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling ribs draft angle.

properties.name: ribLength
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling ribs length measured from the shank axis.

properties.name: ribOffset
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling ribs offset from the top face or alignment face.

properties.name: ribOuterDraftAngle
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling size of rib outer draft angle.

properties.name: ribRotation
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling rotation angle of the first rib from the reference vector. For selected sketch point(s) the direction of reference vector is X-axis of the parent sketch.

properties.name: ribThickness
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling ribs thickness.

properties.name: ribTipRadius
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling size of rib tip blend radius.

properties.name: ribTotalAngle
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling total angle for ribs distribution.

properties.name: ribType
properties.description: Returns the current type of ribs shape this feature represents.

properties.name: rootRadius
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling blend radius of the boss shank.

properties.name: screwDiameter
properties.description: Returns the model parameter for screw diameter - reference for parametric expressions.

properties.name: screwHeadAngle
properties.description: Returns the model parameter for countersink head angle - reference for parametric expressions.

properties.name: screwHeadDiameter
properties.description: Returns the model parameter for screw head diameter - reference for parametric expressions.

properties.name: shapeType
properties.description: Returns the current boss shape this feature represents.

properties.name: taperAngle
properties.description: Returns the model parameter for taper angle - plastic part rule reference.

properties.name: thickness
properties.description: Returns the model parameter for thickness - plastic part rule reference.

properties.name: timelineObject
properties.description: Returns the timeline object associated with this feature.

properties.name: tipRadius
properties.description: Returns the model parameter controlling blend radius of the boss shank top face.

timelineObject.title: BossFeature.timelineObject Property
timelineObject.description: Returns the timeline object associated with this feature.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = bossFeature_var.timelineObject

tipRadius.title: BossFeature.tipRadius Property
tipRadius.description: Returns the model parameter controlling blend radius of the boss shank top face.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = bossFeature_var.tipRadius

classType.title: BossFeature.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.fusion.BossFeature.classType()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::fusion::BossFeature::classType();

classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createForAssemblyContext.title: BossFeature.createForAssemblyContext Method
createForAssemblyContext.description: Creates or returns a proxy for the native object - i.e. a new object that represents this object but adds the assembly context defined by the input occurrence.

syntax.python: returnValue = bossFeature_var.createForAssemblyContext(occurrence)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = bossFeature_var->createForAssemblyContext(occurrence);

createForAssemblyContext.return_description: Returns the proxy object or null if this isn't the NativeObject.

parameters.name: occurrence
parameters.description: The occurrence that defines the context to create the proxy in.

createInput.title: BossFeature.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates object with inputs this feature represents. To use this property, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True).

syntax.python: returnValue = bossFeature_var.createInput()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = bossFeature_var->createInput();

createInput.return_description: Returns BossFeatureInput this feature represent if successful.

deleteMe.title: BossFeature.deleteMe Method
deleteMe.description: Deletes the feature. This works for both parametric and non-parametric features.

syntax.python: returnValue = bossFeature_var.deleteMe()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = bossFeature_var->deleteMe();

deleteMe.return_description: Returns a bool indicating if the delete was successful or not.

dissolve.title: BossFeature.dissolve Method
dissolve.description: Dissolves the feature so that the feature information is lost and only the B-Rep geometry defined by the feature remains. This is only valid for non-parametric features.

syntax.python: returnValue = bossFeature_var.dissolve()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = bossFeature_var->dissolve();

dissolve.return_description: Returns a bool indicating if the dissolve was successful or not.

update.title: BossFeature.update Method
update.description: Changes the boss feature (or boss connection) to the input provided. To use this property, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True).

syntax.python: returnValue = bossFeature_var.update(input)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = bossFeature_var->update(input);

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: The object defines inputs the feature will be set to.
